titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in Codex Colbertinus
VL6
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

surrogates.bibl: Black and white photographs in ITSEE, supplied by Vetus Latina-Institut.

listBibl.bibl: Greyscale scanned microfilm digital images on CD in ITSEE supplied by BNF.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This manuscript has many abbreviations. It has chapter numbers and an initial list of capitula (Type I).

The transcription includes foliation, lineation, rubrication and all punctuation. Most abbreviations have been silently expanded. The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: The prologue to John "Hic est iohannes euangelista, unus ex discipulis dei" is written in the first column of this page.
The prologue finishes on line 6, and the capitula begin on line 8.
The second clause seems to have been added as an afterthought, and the word 'samariam' appears in blank space on the line above.
This word is completed in blank space on the line above.
The gloss is added in blank space on the line above.
The rest of the column is occupied by a portrait of the Evangelist.
A highly-decorated initial 'I' takes up most of the first column.
Remainder of line after testimonium taken up with simple decorative pattern, containing chapter number i
The chapter number ii is enclosed within a simple decorative pattern

At this point, there is a sort of bracket-marking in the text. Another occurs immediately before the beginning of verse 5, and there is a marking in the margin consisting of a capital N with a dot in the lower portion.
The word 'lege' appears in the bottom margin as a catchword for the next page.
The chapter number may be rubricated: the following capital C certainly appears to be in a different colour ink.
